rows.description: Recepta varia
rows.notes: Large centered title at top of page. Letters are slightly stylised but clearly a Latinised heading 'Recepta/Recepta varia'. No amounts present.

rows.description: 1788 Novr. 4th Mr. William Harriott's Plate money
rows.notes: Date and name clearly written at left. Amount columns well aligned with vertical rules; reads 10 0 0 unambiguously.

rows.description: Mr. Bowden's do.
rows.notes: Short description 'Mr. Bowden's do.' (do. = ditto) and amount 10 0 0 aligned under pounds/shillings/pence. Slight abbreviation but amount clear.

rows.description: Dec. 6. of the Revd Mr. Cooke Dr. Hartridge's Benefaction
rows.notes: Date and benefaction description legible. Amount clearly '110 0 0' under the pounds column; alignment is unambiguous.

rows.description: 31 Increment due at Midst last from the Bursar
rows.notes: Description partially faded/abbreviated: read as 'Increment due at Midst last from the Bursar' (some letters uncertain). Amount aligns to '61 | 5 | 2' and there is a small loop after the '2' consistent with the half‑penny 'ob' mark, so pence interpreted as 2 + 1/2. Column alignment used to confirm pence despite faint ink.

rows.description: 1789 Jan. 31. of Mr. Bowles Esq. R/up Rent for A. Witchenham
rows.notes: Date and name readable; 'R/up Rent for A. Witchenham' interpreted from handwriting (some letters ambiguous). Amount '175 0 0' clearly aligned under pounds/shillings/pence.

rows.description: Feb. 24. Geo. King & Mr. John King's Plate money
rows.notes: Date and two names legible. Amount '20 0 0' clearly aligned and unambiguous.

rows.description: Mar. 27. Mr. John Harriott's Plate money
rows.notes: Description and amount '10 0 0' clear; handwriting of name somewhat looped but readable.

rows.description: June 3. of Mr. Cooke Dr. St. Benefac. 2d Payment
rows.notes: Phrase 'Dr. St. Benefac. 2d Payment' reconstructed from abbreviations; amount '110 0 0' is well aligned and clear.

rows.description: 11. of Mr. Parsons 4 years of pews & exhibn which Wm. O'Neill
rows.notes: Description is cramped and some words uncertain: read as 'of Mr. Parsons 4 years of pews & exhibn which Wm. O'Neill'. Amount '12 0 0' is clearly aligned. Lower confidence due to unclear middle phrase.

rows.description: of Mr. Bowles 2d Payment for A. Witchenham
rows.notes: Appears to be a second payment from Mr. Bowles for same rent; wording partly abbreviated. Amount '175 0 0' clearly aligned and unambiguous.

rows.description: 29. of Mr. Demainbray Devis fees to House
rows.notes: Surname read as 'Demainbray' (handwriting uncertain but plausible). Amount '12 0 0' is clear and aligned.

rows.description: July 1. of Mr. Vivian Parson. increm. to Midst. 1789
rows.notes: Name 'Mr. Vivian' readable; phrase 'increm. to Midst. 1789' inferred. Amount columns read '103 | 16 | 5' and a half‑penny loop is visible after the '5', interpreted as 1/2d. Slight uncertainty in description but amounts align well.

rows.description: A Quarter Part of Mr. Donaldson's Time for the share
rows.notes: Phrase read as 'A Quarter Part of Mr. Donaldson's Time for the share' — some words abbreviated and inferred. Amount '26 0 0' is well aligned and clear.

rows.description: Mr. Savandes? Annu. increm. for year 1788
rows.notes: Personal name is uncertain ('Savandes' is an attempt to match handwriting). Amount reads '23 | 17 | 3' with a small half‑penny loop after the '3' indicating 1/2d. Lower confidence due to name uncertainty.

rows.description: oct. 30. of Gibbons, Farmer, for old Iron as by Act.
rows.notes: Date, name 'Gibbons' and purpose 'for old Iron as by Act' are legible. Amount '13 14 0' clearly aligned with the columns.

rows.description: Mr Robt. Hartin Acct. for Merton Estate from Mid. 1787 & Mid. 1788
rows.notes: Centered account heading; wording mostly clear though some abbreviations present (Robt. = Robert, Acct. = account). No amounts on this line.

rows.description: money Rent - 265.0.0.
rows.notes: Component line of the estate account reading 'money Rent - 265.0.0.'; columns align exactly and are unambiguous.

rows.description: Con Rent - 05.12.0.
rows.notes: Second component reads like 'Con Rent' (word uncertain — could be 'Low Rent' or 'Con Rent'); the figures '05.12.0' are clear and aligned as 5 pounds, 12 shillings, 0 pence. Lower confidence for the description word, high confidence for the amounts.

rows.description: Total of years Rent 270.12.0.
rows.notes: Label 'Total of years Rent' is clear and the summed amount '270.12.0' is neatly aligned and unambiguous. Classified as a total per instruction.

rows.description: Deductions allowed
rows.notes: Section heading introducing deduction lines below; no amounts on this line.

rows.description: Land Tax - 34-7-0
rows.notes: Clear printed phrase 'Land Tax' and amount '34-7-0' aligned in pounds/shillings/pence. All values within valid ranges.

rows.description: Leavitth Close - 6-10-10
rows.notes: Description word uncertain ('Leavitth' / 'Leavett' / 'Leawith' etc.) but context indicates a deduction name. Amount '6-10-10' is clear and within valid ranges. Lower confidence due to description uncertainty.

rows.description: Rep. of Vic. House - 7-6-7
rows.notes: Read as 'Rep. of Vic. House' (Repair of Vicarage/Parsonage House). Amount '7-6-7' is aligned and legible. Slight abbreviation causes modest uncertainty in the description.

rows.description: Dr. of Chancel - 0-8-9
rows.notes: Interpreted as 'Dr. of Chancel' (likely 'Disbursements of Chancel' or 'Ditto of Chancel'); amount '0-8-9' clear and aligned.

rows.description: Total of Deductions 48-12-4
rows.notes: Subtotal for deductions. The text and amount '48-12-4' are clearly aligned and consistent with the items above; classified as a total.

rows.description: Remains clear for the Year's Rent 221 19 8
rows.notes: Phrasing 'Remains clear for the Year's Rent' is visible (some flourishes). Amount '221 19 8' is clearly written and aligned; this is a resulting balance so classified as a total.
Bottom-right grand total reading '1094 12 7' with a small half‑penny loop after the '7' interpreted as 1/2d. The figure is slightly cramped and the halfpenny mark is small, hence moderate confidence.
